title: AMD details Ryzen AI 400 desktop with up to 8 cores, Radeon 860M graphics APUs won t be available as boxed units, only in OEM systems
summary: After announcing Ryzen AI 400 APUs earlier this year, AMD has finally revealed the specifications for the chips. However, they re targeting OEM systems, and they top out lower than AMD s Ryzen AI 400 mobile lineup.

title: Lenovo's Legion Go Fold-able gaming handheld concept has four screen modes, also works as a small laptop POLED display unfolds from 7.7 to 11.6 inches
summary: Lenovo's Legion Go Fold handheld concept features four screen modes and doubles as a small laptop, with a POLED display unfolding from 7.7 to 11.6 inches. It utilizes Intel Core Ultra Series 2 processors and detachable controllers. The prototype requires further stability improvements.
insight: Revolutionizing gaming and portability with foldable display technology

title: Lenovo's Framework-like Modular AI PC concept lets you put a screen just about anywhere detachable keyboard and ports make the machine customizable
summary: Lenovo's ThinkBook Modular AI PC Concept features a detachable display and keyboard for customization.
insight: Flexible design enables users to tailor their device to specific tasks.

title: Lenovo brings a blue ThinkPad T14 to Mobile World Congress slew of new devices includes a Legion 7a with Strix Halo
summary: Lenovo is updating its ThinkPad, Yoga, and Legion laptop lines at Mobile World Congress in Barcelona, featuring new devices including a blue ThinkPad T14 and a Legion 7a with Strix Halo.
insight: Lenovo expands its laptop lineup with innovative features and designs.

title: Gigabyte's huge 55-inch 4K 120Hz gaming monitor is now half price grab this Android-powered display with HDMI 2.1 for just $9 per inch
summary: Gigabyte's S55U is a 55-inch 4K 120Hz gaming monitor with Android-powered capabilities.
insight: High-end gaming monitor now affordable for a wider audience.

title: Windows 11 continues gaining traction, nears 75% market share Windows 10 finally on the way out, some five months after Microsoft axes support
summary: Windows 11 surpasses 70% market share after Microsoft ends Windows 10 support. This milestone comes five months after Microsoft announced the end of support for Windows 10. The shift indicates growing adoption of the newer operating system.
insight: Microsoft's strategic support phase-out boosts Windows 11 adoption

title: Amiga Workbench Simulator helps you pick your ultimate retro desktop TAWS recently updated with refinements to OS 3.2, AmiBench presets
summary: TAWS, an Amiga Workbench simulator, has been updated to refine Workbench 3.2 and AmiBench, enhancing the user experience.
insight: Immersive retro computing experience now more realistic and detailed.

title: 200,000 living human neurons on a microchip demonstrated playing Doom Cortical Labs CL1 video shows the gameplay and explains how the neurons learn the game
summary: Cortical Labs' CL1 biological computer demonstrated 200,000 living neurons playing Doom, showcasing neural learning capabilities.
insight: Advancements in artificial brains could revolutionize AI and machine learning.

title: Orico BookDrive P10Plus (512GB) review: Magnetic back and 100W passthrough charging
summary: Orico's BookDrive P10Plus offers a solid construction, magnetic attachment, and 100W passthrough charging.
insight: Performance lacking for professional users in one key area slows productivity.

title: Enthusiast runs desktop PC off 56 AA batteries Intel computer lasts less than 5 minutes while playing Minesweeper
summary: A desktop PC powered by 56 AA batteries lasted less than 5 minutes while playing Minesweeper, showcasing the significant power consumption of modern computers.
insight: Desktop PCs consume massive power, limiting battery life significantly.

title: The Nvidia GeForce3 launched 25 years ago underappreciated at launch, its impact shaped the industry
summary: The Nvidia GeForce3, launched 25 years ago, was initially underappreciated but significantly impacted the industry. Its innovative design and performance paved the way for future graphics cards. The GeForce3's influence can still be seen in modern gaming and graphics technology.
insight: Innovation often appears insignificant in its initial, groundbreaking moment.

title: California introduces age verification law for all operating systems, including Linux and SteamOS user age verified during OS account setup
summary: California introduces a law requiring age verification for all OS providers, including Linux and SteamOS, as part of the user account setup process. This broad definition aims to prevent minors from accessing online content and services. The law affects a wide range of operating systems and their providers.
insight: Age verification expands to cover diverse, previously unregulated operating systems.

title: AMD s K6-III Sharptooth debuted this week in 1999 with on-die L2 cache to savage the Intel Pentium II it also held the line against the Pentium III
summary: AMD launched its first processors with on-die L2 cache in 1999, marking the beginning of the Super Socket 7 era.
insight: Innovative design led to improved performance and competitiveness with Intel processors.

title: Lenovo ThinkEdge SE60n Gen 2 fanless edge AI computer features up to 97 TOPS Intel Core Ultra 7 265H SoC
summary: Lenovo unveiled the ThinkEdge SE60n Gen 2, a fanless edge AI computer powered by up to an Intel Core Ultra 7 265H SoC, offering up to 97 TOPS of AI performance and various connectivity options.
insight: Advancements in edge AI enable faster, more efficient data processing in real-world applications.

title: CHUWI CoreBook Air Plus 16 review Part 3: Ubuntu 25.10 on a mid-range AMD Ryzen 5 6600H laptop
summary: The reviewer tests the CHUWI CoreBook Air Plus 16 with Ubuntu 25.10, evaluating system performance, benchmarks, video playback, feature testing, storage, WiFi 6 performance, fan noise, and power consumption.
insight: Mid-range laptops with AMD Ryzen 5 can run Linux smoothly and efficiently.

title: Windows 11 hits 72% share as Windows 10 fades, but not everyone is happy
summary: Windows 11 has surpassed 72% market share, largely driven by expiring support deadlines and hardware policies. This shift appears to be more of a necessity than a voluntary upgrade. Many users are unhappy with the transition experience.
insight: Forced Windows adoption driven by expiring support deadlines and hardware policies.

title: Google is rolling out quantum-resistant HTTPS certificates in Chrome to future-proof the web
summary: Google is introducing quantum-resistant HTTPS certificates to secure websites against potential hacks.
insight: Enhancing web security to thwart quantum computer-based cyber threats.

title: Google is building a Minnesota data center powered by wind, solar, and rust
summary: Google is building a Minnesota data center powered by 1.9 gigawatts of carbon-free electricity from wind and solar infrastructure, marking a significant renewable energy achievement in the industry.
insight: Google's solution relies on innovative energy storage technology.
